name: SAW / TC-SAW Filters
description: Sub-sector template for Surface Acoustic Wave (SAW) and Temperature-Compensated SAW (TC-SAW) filter patents. Covers IDT electrode design, waveguide structures, temperature compensation layers, and SAW-based filter/duplexer integration.
contextDescription: SAW filters dominate the low-frequency RF filter market (below ~2.5 GHz). TC-SAW extends SAW to higher performance by adding temperature compensation layers (SiO2). Every smartphone uses 10-30 SAW/TC-SAW filters for LTE/sub-6 bands. Key vendors: Murata (dominant), TDK, Qualcomm (via TDK partnership), Skyworks, Qorvo. SAW technology competes with BAW at mid-frequencies (1.5-2.5 GHz). Key innovations: I.H.P. SAW (incredibly high performance), guided SAW, and multi-layer piezoelectric substrates.
scoringGuidance: SAW filters are in every smartphone — 10-30 per device for different frequency bands.
TC-SAW patents are high-value because they enable premium filter performance at lower cost than BAW.
I.H.P. SAW and guided SAW patents represent next-generation technology.
IDT (interdigital transducer) electrode design patents are fundamental — every SAW device has IDTs.
Multi-layer piezoelectric substrates (bonded wafer) are enabling higher frequency SAW operation.

questions.question: What SAW technology variant does this patent address? Score 8-10 for I.H.P. SAW, guided SAW, or novel SAW architectures, 6-7 for TC-SAW (temperature compensated), 4-5 for standard SAW with IDT improvements, 2-3 for general acoustic wave, 1 for unrelated.
questions.reasoningPrompt: I.H.P. SAW and guided SAW are the next-gen variants with highest technology value.

questions.question: Does this patent address temperature stability in SAW devices (SiO2 layers, material engineering, TCF optimization)? Score 8-10 for novel temperature compensation techniques, 5-7 for TC-SAW layer optimization, 3-4 for temperature awareness, 1-2 for no temperature aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Temperature stability is the key differentiator for TC-SAW over standard SAW.

questions.question: Does this patent address interdigital transducer (IDT) electrode design (weighting, apodization, electrode material, pitch optimization)? Score 8-10 for novel electrode architectures, 5-7 for electrode optimization, 3-4 for basic IDT configuration, 1-2 for no IDT aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: IDT design is fundamental to every SAW device — electrode patterns define performance.

questions.question: Does this patent involve piezoelectric substrate innovation (lithium niobate, lithium tantalate, bonded wafer, thin-film piezo on silicon)? Score 8-10 for novel substrate architectures (bonded wafer, thin-film), 5-7 for substrate orientation/cut optimization, 3-4 for standard substrates, 1-2 for no substrate focus.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Bonded wafer and thin-film piezo substrates enable higher frequency SAW operation.

questions.question: How detectable is this SAW innovation via physical analysis? Score 8-10 for IDT patterns visible in optical/SEM, 5-7 for layer stacks detectable via cross-section, 3-4 for material composition via EDS/XRD, 1-2 for design methodology only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: SAW IDT patterns and layer stacks are readily visible in physical analysis.

questions.question: Which frequency bands does this SAW technology target? Score 8-10 for bands where SAW competes with BAW (1.5-2.7 GHz), 5-7 for traditional SAW bands (700 MHz-1.5 GHz), 3-4 for sub-700 MHz, 1-2 for no frequency specificity.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Higher frequency SAW patents have more competitive value — they overlap with BAW territory.
